techStack: Node.js + Express + TypeScript backend with vanilla HTML/CSS/JS frontend

files.purpose: Node.js dependencies and scripts for backend and dev server
TypeScript compiler configuration for backend
Express server entry point, API routes, and middleware setup
SQLite database initialization, schema, and query helpers
Habit data model, CRUD operations, and streak calculation logic
User data model, authentication helpers, and messaging preferences
Cron-based reminder scheduling and execution engine
Unified interface for SMS, Telegram, and WhatsApp delivery
SMS delivery via Twilio with rate limit handling
Telegram Bot API integration with retry logic
WhatsApp Business API integration with fallback handling
REST API endpoints for habit CRUD and completion tracking
REST API endpoints for user registration and preferences
REST API endpoints for progress data and streak statistics
Centralized error handling and logging middleware
Environment variables, API keys, and platform configuration
Main web UI with habit list, forms, and navigation structure
Responsive layout, mobile-first design, and visual styling
Frontend logic for habit management, API calls, and UI updates
Progress chart rendering using Canvas API with 30-day view
Template for required environment variables and API credentials
Setup instructions, API documentation, and deployment guide

runCommand: npm install && npm run build && npm start
constraints: Use SQLite for local data persistence to minimize external dependencies
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for all messaging provider calls
Store API credentials in environment variables, never in code
Handle timezone conversions server-side using UTC storage
Implement rate limit tracking per provider to avoid quota exhaustion
Use polling fallback if webhook delivery fails for messaging platforms
Validate phone numbers and messaging IDs before storing user preferences
Cache chart data for 5 minutes to reduce database load
Log all messaging delivery attempts with status for debugging
Implement graceful degradation if one messaging provider is unavailable
